User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Script Canvas application. This entity stores core user profile information and is linked to the projects they create.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: A unique, user-friendly name chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for account recovery and communication.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a creative story project, such as a screenplay, novel, or short story, created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this project. (Relationship: User 1:N Project)

title.description: The main title of the story project.

logline.description: A concise one-sentence summary of the project's core conflict.

synopsis.description: A brief summary of the project's plot and themes.

genre.description: The genre of the story project (e.g., 'Sci-Fi', 'Fantasy', 'Drama').

status.description: The current development status of the project (e.g., 'Draft', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the project was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the project was last updated.

Scene.title: Scene
Scene.description: Represents an individual scene within a story project, containing its content and metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Scene entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this scene belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Scene)

title.description: A brief descriptive title for the scene.

content.description: The actual script or narrative text for the scene.

orderIndex.description: The chronological order index of the scene within its project.

locationIds.description: References to Locations featured in this scene. (Relationship: Scene N:N Location)

characterIds.description: References to Characters present in this scene. (Relationship: Scene N:N Character)

notes.description: Additional notes or ideas for the scene.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the scene was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the scene was last updated.

Character.title: Character
Character.description: Represents a character within a story project, detailing their profile, backstory, and motivations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Character entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this character belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Character)

name.description: The full name of the character.

description.description: A physical description and personality overview of the character.

backstory.description: The character's past experiences and history.

motivations.description: The driving forces behind the character's actions.

goals.description: What the character aims to achieve in the story.

notes.description: Additional notes or ideas about the character.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the character profile was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the character profile was last updated.

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Represents a specific setting or place within a story project's world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this location belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Location)

description.description: A detailed description of the location, including sensory details.

type.description: The classification of the location (e.g., 'Indoor', 'Outdoor', 'Fantasy City', 'Spaceship').

notes.description: Additional notes or ideas about the location.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the location was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the location was last updated.

WorldElement.title: WorldElement
WorldElement.description: Represents an abstract or conceptual element of a story project's world-building (e.g., magic systems, historical events, political structures).

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldElement entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this world element belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N WorldElement)

name.description: The name or title of the world element.

type.description: The classification of the world element (e.g., 'Magic System', 'Faction', 'Historical Event', 'Technology').

description.description: A detailed explanation of the world element and its implications.

notes.description: Additional notes or ideas about the world element.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the world element was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the world element was last updated.

PlotPoint.title: PlotPoint
PlotPoint.description: Represents a significant event or turning point in a story project's narrative, used for timeline visualization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlotPoint entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this plot point belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N PlotPoint)

title.description: A concise title for the plot point.

description.description: A detailed description of what happens at this plot point and its significance.

timelinePosition.description: The chronological position of the plot point on the narrative timeline.

sceneIds.description: References to Scenes associated with this plot point. (Relationship: PlotPoint N:N Scene)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the plot point was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the plot point was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) matching the `userId` in the path.
Stores user-owned creative projects. The `userId` field within the document must match the `userId` in the path and `request.auth.uid` for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific project.

definition.description: Stores individual scenes belonging to a specific project. Includes denormalized `userId` from the parent project for authorization independence and `projectId` for direct linking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project and, by extension, this scene. Denormalized for security rules.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project this scene belongs to.

params.name: sceneId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific scene.

definition.description: Stores character profiles for a specific project. Includes denormalized `userId` from the parent project for authorization independence and `projectId` for direct linking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project and this character. Denormalized for security rules.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project this character belongs to.

params.name: characterId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific character.

definition.description: Stores location details for a specific project. Includes denormalized `userId` from the parent project for authorization independence and `projectId` for direct linking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project and this location. Denormalized for security rules.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project this location belongs to.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific location.

definition.description: Stores world-building elements for a specific project. Includes denormalized `userId` from the parent project for authorization independence and `projectId` for direct linking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project and this world element. Denormalized for security rules.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project this world element belongs to.

params.name: worldElementId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific world element.

definition.description: Stores plot points for a project's timeline visualization. Includes denormalized `userId` from the parent project for authorization independence and `projectId` for direct linking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the project and this plot point. Denormalized for security rules.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project this plot point belongs to.

params.name: plotPointId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific plot point.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable database.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
Each subcollection document (`Scene`, `Character`, `Location`, `WorldElement`, `PlotPoint`) will denormalize the `userId` from its parent `Project` and the owning `User`. This means that every document in these subcollections will explicitly contain a `userId` field that matches the `userId` present in the document's path (e.g., `projects/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/scenes/{sceneId}` will have `scene.userId` == `userId`). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, as authorization can be verified solely by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for any document. This allows for atomic writes and updates across projects and their sub-entities without complex rule dependencies, making transactions and batch operations simpler and more reliable.

For example, to create a new scene for a project, the client would write to `/users/{current_uid}/projects/{projectId}/scenes/{sceneId}`. The scene document itself would contain `userId: current_uid` and `projectId: projectId`. The security rule would simply verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and potentially `resource.data.projectId == projectId` (though the path itself already implies this).

**Structural Segregation and QAPs (Query as Authorization Predicate):**
All data is segregated by user (`/users/{userId}/...`), ensuring that one user's data is never mixed with another's. Within a user's tree, project-specific data is further segregated into subcollections (e.g., `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/scenes`). This homogeneous security posture means all documents within a given collection or subcollection share the same authorization requirements – they are owned by the `userId` in the path. This simplifies security rules significantly, as a single rule can apply to an entire collection, focusing on `request.auth.uid` matching the `userId` in the path and/or the denormalized `userId` field within the document.

This structure inherently supports QAPs. When a user queries for their projects (e.g., `db.collection('users/{userId}/projects')`), the `userId` in the path naturally filters the data to only documents belonging to the authenticated user. Similarly, when querying for scenes within a project (e.g., `db.collection('users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}/scenes')`), the path constraint, combined with the denormalized `userId` in the scene documents, provides robust and efficient security without requiring server-side filtering after data retrieval. This guarantees that `list` operations are secure and only return data the user is authorized to see, directly aligning with the QAPs principle.
